instruction: Leave the bedroom and take a right in the hall, and walk straight. Take a left into the third entry way on your left and enter the bedroom. Stop in the doorway to the bedroom.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.
I'm stuck in this bedroom and need to find a way out—let me look around and see if there's a door I've missed.
I'm hitting a wall here, so I'll keep exploring.
I'm looking at a hallway ahead and considering whether to explore the full room layout by turning around more, or just move forward through what appears to be the exit.
I'm back in the bedroom now, so I need to retrace my steps and figure out which direction leads back to the hallway exit.

We completed a full 360-degree rotation in 24 steps, so the hallway view appeared at step 18, which corresponds to a 270-degree left turn from the starting position. To return to that same view, I need to turn right 90 degrees (since 360 minus 270 equals 90), which would give me the same perspective as the 270-degree left turn.

I'm tracking the pattern of views as I move through this space—at 6 steps left I see a wall closeup, at 12 steps it's the window view, at 18 steps the hallway appears, and at 24 steps I'm back at the starting corner. Now I'm checking what happens at 30 steps when moving right from the start.

The math suggests that 24 left steps should complete a full rotation, then 6 right steps would put me at the equivalent of 18 left steps from the original heading, which should show the hallway view. But instead I'm seeing the window view again, like what appeared at step 12—there's a discrepancy of about one turn count. Either my angle assumptions are off or the observed images have subtle differences I'm not catching. I'll keep turning right to locate the hallway view and figure out what's happening.
I'm noticing a mirrored wardrobe and a doorway to the right, so I'll keep turning that direction to locate the hallway exit.
We're back in the hallway now, looking out from the bedroom doorway. Let me step through and continue forward.
I'm in the hallway now and need to follow the instruction to turn right. Let me move forward a bit to see if there's a right turn ahead.
I'm continuing down the hallway, keeping an eye out for a right turn ahead.
I'm noticing a curved wall on the left and a doorway ahead—this could be where the path turns. Let me explore to the right to see what options are available there.

I'm looking more carefully at the layout—there's a doorway to the right, but the instructions mention taking a right in the hall itself, suggesting the corridor continues and turns rather than just opening into another room. Let me explore further to the right to find where the hallway actually bends.

I've made a 90-degree turn from my original direction, and now I'm seeing a hallway extending to the left, which suggests I may have misinterpreted the layout or the instructions about turning right from the bedroom.

Let me reconsider the sequence: the instruction says to exit the bedroom, take a right in the hall, and walk straight. This probably means I should turn right immediately upon entering the hallway from the bedroom door, not after walking some distance. Then I'd walk straight down that hallway, and later take a left into the third doorway on my left. I've been walking straight for several steps now without encountering a right junction, just seeing doors on both sides of the corridor, so I may need to reconsider whether I've already made the right turn or if it's still ahead.
This matches the view from step 43, so I'm back at the original hall-facing direction. Now I'll move forward through the doorway ahead.
Moving forward toward the doorway.
I'm at the doorway threshold with a wall ahead and a corridor branching to the right—this is where the hallway turns, so I'll head right to explore where it leads.
There's a hallway junction ahead with doors on either side—a white one to the left and a wood-colored one on the right. I should turn right to get a better view of the full corridor.

I'm in the main hallway now with a railing on the right and doors lining the left side—this matches the right turn instruction. I need to continue forward and count the doorways on the left to find the third entryway.
I notice a tan-colored door on the left side ahead, so I'll keep moving forward.
I'm noting the first entryway on the left with the tan door, and I should continue forward to explore what other doorways are ahead.
We're moving through the doorway now, passing the tan door that's opened to reveal a room on the left, and continuing forward.
I can see a room opening on the left side—looks like it might be a bathroom with a tub—which marks the first entryway. Now I'm moving forward to identify the next ones.
I'm moving past the bathroom entrance on the left and continuing down the hallway.

I can see a doorway opening up on the right with what looks like a furnished room inside, and there might be another entrance coming up on the left side. I should keep moving forward to explore further.

I should check the left side of the corridor for any doorways I might be missing, but since the camera is forward-facing, doorways on the left will naturally appear in the left portion of the image as we move through the hallway. I'll keep watching carefully as we continue forward.
I'm noticing a wall ahead, so let me check what's to the left and right to get a sense of the space around me.
I need to go back and review the previous section more carefully, then also examine what's on the right side.
I'm noticing a bedroom entrance on my right with a bed visible, and I'm realizing I may have already wandered into a room. Let me turn right to get a better view of the full hallway layout.

I'm getting a clearer picture of the space now—there's a hallway with a staircase railing on the right, a bedroom entrance on the left with a bed visible, and a double-door closet in the center. Let me continue exploring the hallway to the right to see what else is there.
I'm at what appears to be the end of the hallway with a railing overlooking a stairwell, so I should explore whether the passage continues to the right or if this is a dead end.

The hallway continues with a tan door visible on the right, so I'm deciding whether to keep rotating to get a full 360-degree view or move forward—I think turning right more will give me a better sense of the space.

I'm retracing my steps through this path—at step 84 I hit a dead end facing the wall, then turned left to explore but found blank space. From there I turned right multiple times: 6 steps got me back to the bedroom view, then another 3 steps right at step 93, so I'm trying to figure out my total rotation and position.

Actually, this is getting too tangled with all the turning calculations. I should simplify my approach: go back to the main corridor and walk forward methodically, only turning to peek at doorways when I notice them, rather than doing all these exploratory turns. Let me reorient by finding the corridor again—I can look for distinctive features like the railing or the floor extending forward to figure out which way I'm facing, then turn left gradually while observing to get my bearings.
I'm recognizing this as the same corridor view from step 99, with the railing on the left and a tan door ahead, so I'll continue forward down the hallway in this natural direction.
The corridor stretches ahead with a railing on the left side and a door approaching on the right—I'm moving forward through this space.

I've passed the tan door that was on my right and is now behind me. The railing continues along the left side, suggesting this is an open stairwell landing or long balcony area. I'll keep moving forward.
I see a doorway opening up ahead on the right side, so I'll keep moving forward.

I'm approaching a wall with a doorway to the right, which could be the end of this hallway. Since the instructions mention checking left turns for entryways, let me explore that direction first to see what's there.
I'm noticing a bed in the room to my right, and there's a dark doorway opening on the left side—this looks like a junction point. Let me explore that left passage.
I'm noticing the railing again and a narrow doorway coming up on the left, so I should angle my movement more in that direction.
I'm looking at the railing and stairwell area where the hallway curves, so I need to pan the view further left to see what comes next.
